title: Kit-free automated RNA extraction for SARS-CoV-2 testing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"edghp","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5c3n","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"4tuk7","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bdo89","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"dr2g2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"h60t","text":"Purpose of examination / Clinical relevance","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":43}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4on8q","text":"At the end of 2019, several pneumonia cases were reported in Wuhan, China and the pathogen was confirmed as a new viral strain. World Health organization has named the newly identified coronavirus as 2019-nCoV, also known as SARS-Cov-2. The disease developed into a dangerous pandemic, posing major challenges to the NHS. Although more research is necessary to better understand the virus, in response to the emergency, simple and rapid testing is essential to identify the virus in infected individuals. This will aid the implementation of efficient interventions to contain the spread, and distinguish healthcare workers who have been infected, and are required to self-isolate, from those showing similar symptoms but which are not 2019-nCoV associated. The latter category may continue to work, alleviating stress on hard-pressed healthcare resources. 2019-nCoV is an RNA virus, and the diagnostic tests detect viral RNA in swabs from patient airways using a reverse transcriptase PCR assay. Samples are submitted to HSL, an accredited reporting laboratory, and transferred to the Crick for testing. The first step of the process is sample receipt at the Crick. This SOP describes the extraction of RNA from the inactivated sample.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1o7qu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d2ubl","text":"Principles of Examination","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c04hm","text":"This procedure involves extraction of COVID-19 RNA from inactivated virus samples that have been aliquoted into plates. This protocol follows a method developed in-house that is automated on the Biomek FX liquid handling platform. All reagents and buffers prepared in house are batch tested and certified.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":56,"length":25},{"key":3,"offset":97,"length":21}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"notes","mutability":"","data":{"blocks":[{"data":[],"depth":0,"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"length":38,"offset":29}],"inlineStyleRanges":[],"key":"4r9td","text":"This protocol is part of the Crick COVID-19 RT-PCR Testing Pipeline collection.","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{"0":{"data":{"guid":"D6310A7096FA11EA9D3D59602BA73FC5","url":"https://www.protocols.io/private/9BA5EEFF847911EA9B0C0242AC110002"},"mutability":"","type":"link"}}}},"1":{"type":"safety","mutability":"","data":{"blocks":[{"data":[],"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[{"length":101,"offset":0,"style":"bold"}],"key":"33p8b","text":"THIS DOCUMENT MUST BE READ AND UNDERSTOOD BY STAFF USING IT AND DOCUMENTED EVIDENCE PROVIDED THEREOF.","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"AC0715B096F911EA9D3D59602BA73FC5","url":"https://www.protocols.io/private/E84AE35681AE11EABF5D0242AC110002"}},"3":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"D24064C096F911EA9D3D59602BA73FC5","url":"https://www.protocols.io/private/0B4B016082F611EABF5D0242AC110002"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"7tqrc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"549vr","text":" ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":[]},{"key":"vean","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7m69a","text":"Grade of operator","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6g5mh","text":"All qualified members of staff who have been signed off as competent and supervised trainees.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7d0ss","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5rvj2","text":"Disposal","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1po4a","text":"If the need arises to dispose of tubes or plates (e.g. due to spillage), all contaminated tubes and reagents used are discarded into plastic disposable jars and then into autoclave waste bins. All sharps are placed in a sharps container prior to incineration.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3pfui","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5duie","text":"Automation","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3kcfu","text":"This protocol describes the automation of this method on the Biomek FX liquid handling platform but it could be performed manually or programmed on other instruments. We have also installed this method on an Agilent Bravo and obrain the same performance.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"safety","mutability":"","data":{"blocks":[{"data":[],"depth":0,"entityRanges":[],"inlineStyleRanges":[{"length":101,"offset":0,"style":"bold"}],"key":"33p8b","text":"THIS DOCUMENT MUST BE READ AND UNDERSTOOD BY STAFF USING IT AND DOCUMENTED EVIDENCE PROVIDED THEREOF.","type":"unstyled"}],"entityMap":{}}}}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"dsp77","text":"Health and Safety","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":17}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6hlb","text":"All practices must be carried out in accordance with the current health and safety policies and procedures. If in any doubt about the aspects of health and safety concerning this procedure, seek advice from the departmental Safety Officer or the health and safety team.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7gg1p","text":"Guanidine hydrochloride is harmful if inhaled, swallowed or if it comes into contact with skin and causes severe skin burns and eye damage. SARS-CoV-2 is currently classified as a Hazard Group Three (HG3) agent by Advisory Committee on Dangerous Pathogens (ACDP). As such this work falls within the Control of Substances Hazardous to Health (COSHH) Regulations.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"erurp","text":"The Government has provided guidance for clinical diagnostic laboratories safety, sampling and packaging of specimens associated with COVID-19. For hazards, risks and appropriate control measures identified in the risk assessment relevant to this procedure.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bvvir","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c3t42","text":"PPE","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cere","text":"General personal protective equipment (PPE) Control Measures for laboratory work include the wearing of closed toe footwear, laboratory coat, appropriate disposable gloves (nitrile for general work or specified gloves for chemical work), and safety spectacles should be worn throughout this procedure.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"25vag","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1j2g4","text":"Spillage","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":8}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cf0vl","text":"The spill kits provided for use in the department can be used for both biohazard and chemical spills. If a spill does occur, follow the procedure within the spill kit.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: RNA extraction automated on Biomek FX using IN HOUSE protocol. This protocol descibes the processing of one 96-well plate (93 samples and 3 controls). [Note: At the Crick we have two platforms with slightly different decks (FX1 and FX2) and this protocol descibes setup on FX2] Open the Biomek software, and select the program “RNA Extraction vFINAL” which is located in the “Covid-19” project:
Click on “Instrument Setup” as shown below to view the deck layout: FX2 DECK LAYOUT
Place 4 boxes (lid off) of 250 μl filter tips (green boxes) as shown above.
Place 3 boxes (lid off) of 50 μl filter tips (pink boxes) as shown above.
Place an Axygen Deep well plate in position P12.
At the bench, prepare Beads , Binding buffer/bead mix , PE Buffer , TET Buffer , Sample Plate , Elution Plate and Magnetic Plate as follows:

Take a 1.5 ml tube of pre-aliquoted silica beads ( 550 µL ) from the fridge and vortex well. We routinely use Silica Magnetic Beads from G-BioScience (see Materials ) but we have also tested and validated SeraSil Mag 400 beads (GE Healthcare/Cytiva) as a potential substitite.
Record beads manufacturer and LOT number.
Place on the single tube magnet for 00:01:00 or until supernatant is clear.
Remove and discard supernatant (into the tip waste bin).
Remove tube from magnet and add 550 µL, TET . Resuspend the beads by vortexing.
Pulse spin the tube and place on the magnet for 00:01:00 or until supernatant is clear.
Remove and discard supernatant.
Repeat bead washing for a total of 3 times.
Add 550 µL, TET . Resuspend the beads by vortexing. Pulse spin the tube and place on the magnet for 00:01:00 or until supernatant is clear. Remove and discard supernatant. (2/3)
Add 550 µL, TET . Resuspend the beads by vortexing. Pulse spin the tube and place on the magnet for 00:01:00 or until supernatant is clear. Remove and discard supernatant. (3/3)
Remove the tube from the magnet, resuspend the beads in 550 µL, TET , vortex and spin down.
In the Falcon tube containing the 50 mL, binding buffer (labelled BB, pre-aliquoted) add the beads prepared..
Mix well by inverting.
Pour into a reservoir and aliquot 400 µL immediately in each well of a 96 deep well Nunc plate, using a multichannel pipette. Place on the deck in position P9 .
Add PE buffer to a new reservoir and aliquot 600 µL into a new Nunc plate and place plate in position P8 .
Add TET to new reservoir and aliquot 06 mL, TET (from batch certified aliquot) into a new Nunc plate and place in position P15 .
Place the sample plate (seal removed) in position P10 . N.B.: If samples have been temporarily stored, briefly spin in the plate centrifuge.
Label an empty Eppendorf LoBind 96-well skirted plate with a pre-printed SPL barcode (e.g. SPL00000) on the front side, and place in position P16 .
Place the Alpaqua Magnum FLX magnetic plate in position P13 .
The final set up should look like the photo below: FX2 DECK LAYOUT
Using the laptop beside the FX, push the samples through the Extraction step in ClarityLIMS (refer to ‘COVID-19 LIMS walkthrough’ document on COVID-19 sample handling landing page).
Make sure you switch ON the Inheco (blue box next to the FX monitor).

To START the program press the Run button (green triangle) located in the software. The robot carries out the following automated protocol 1. Transfer 150 μl of samples to 400 μl (2.6x Vol) of Binding Buffer (BB) containing the Silica beads and mixe 10 times by pipetting up and down 2. Move the plate on the Shaker for 5 min 3. Move plate to position P8 and mix 20 times 4. Move plate to Shaker for 2 min 5. Move plate to P8 and incubate for 3 min 6. Move plate to Magnet for 5 min 7. Remove supernatant 8. Move plate from magnet to position P8 Wash with PE (1) 9. Transfer 180 μl of PE Buffer in each well of the Bead Mixture plate 10. Move plate to the Shaker for 20 sec 11. Move plate to the Magnet for 60 sec 12. Remove supernatant 13. Move plate from the magnet to position P8 Wash with PE (2) 14. Transfer 180 μl of PE Buffer in each well of the Bead Mixture plate 15. Move plate to the Shaker for 20 sec 16. Move plate to the Magnet for 60 sec 17. Remove supernatant 18. Move plate from the magnet to position P8 Wash with PE (3) 19. Transfer 180 μl of PE Buffer in each well of the Bead Mixture plate 20. Move plate to the Shaker for 20 sec 21. Move plate to the Magnet for 60 sec 22. Remove supernatant 23. Move plate from the magnet to position P8 24. Wait for 2 min (drying) Elution 25. Transfer 40 μl of TET Buffer to the Bead Mixture plate and mix 10 times 26. Move plate to the Shaker for 5 min 27. Move plate to the magnet for 60 sec 28. Transfer 35 μl from the Bead Mixture plate to the Eppendorf twin.tec plate
When then program is complete, remove the Eppendorf 96-well skirted plate from the deck, and seal with a Thermo Adhesive PCR plate seal.
Continue with the qPCR RNA Transfer or place in the -80 °C until it is used for qPCR setup.

Archiving of Inactivated viral samples would have already been performed after sample plating . For archiving the stock RNA plates using the app in Clarity LIMS, a database table is updated with the information.
This results in an entry appearing on the dashboard to let the team know the samples are ready to be archived:
An archiving app then displays the boxes and plates that are waiting to be archived, and gives the team the chance to enter the storage information:
Once logged, the record disappears from this screen, and the archiving information will appear in the archive search app:
creator: Jerome Nicod
